---
title: "MTH 4330 Linear Model"
subtitle: "Linear Model for Group Project"
author: "Raymond Cheung"
date: "2026-05-07"
output: pdf_document
header-includes:
# Add any LaTeX packages you want here -----------------------
- \usepackage{amsmath}
- \usepackage{amssymb}
- \usepackage{graphicx}
---



<!-- Keep this block  -->
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

# Add any R packages you want here -----------------------
library(rstudioapi)

library(dplyr) #added

library(xtable) #added to export latex tables

# Do not touch below this line ---------------------------
setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
def.chunk.hook  <- knitr::knit_hooks$get("chunk")
knitr::knit_hooks$set(chunk = function(x, options) {
  x <- def.chunk.hook(x, options)
  paste0("\n \\", "footnotesize","\n\n", x, "\n\n \\normalsize")
})
```

```{r install_libraries, echo = TRUE}
#install the library dplyr
#source: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/54621706/error-in-librarydplyr-there-is-no-package-called-dplyr

#comment this out if you already installed it
#install.packages("dplyr")

# something similar for installing xtable
#install.packages("xtable")

```

```{r linear_regression_analysis, echo = TRUE}

# Note: the dev set was not used for the linear model. It was used with the random forest model mainly to select hyperparameters. We keep the same train/dev/test split for better comparability.

# this entire thing is taken from Random_forest.R from the GitHub. Slight modification: centering fiscal year

library(data.table)
library(ranger)

payroll <- data.table::fread(
  "prorated_annual_Citywide_Payroll_Data_(Fiscal_Year)_20260227.csv",
  select = c(
    "Fiscal Year",
    "Regular Gross Paid",
    "Total OT Paid",
    "Base Salary",
    "Regular Hours",
    "OT Hours",
    "Pay Basis"
  )
)

payroll <- payroll[toupper(trimws(`Pay Basis`)) == "PRORATED ANNUAL"]

payroll$`Regular Gross Paid` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Regular Gross Paid`)))
payroll$`Total OT Paid` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Total OT Paid`)))
payroll$`Total OT Paid`[is.na(payroll$`Total OT Paid`)] <- 0

payroll$Total_Paid <- payroll$`Regular Gross Paid` + payroll$`Total OT Paid`

payroll$`Fiscal Year` <- as.numeric(as.character(payroll$`Fiscal Year`)) - 2014 # centered at 2014 = 0
payroll$`Regular Hours` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Regular Hours`)))
payroll$`Base Salary` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`Base Salary`)))
payroll$`OT Hours` <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", as.character(payroll$`OT Hours`)))

payroll <- payroll[, c(
  "Fiscal Year",
  "Regular Hours",
  "Base Salary",
  "OT Hours",
  "Total_Paid"
)]

payroll <- na.omit(payroll)

names(payroll) <- c("Fiscal_Year", "Regular_Hours", "Base_Salary", "OT_Hours", "Total_Paid")

set.seed(42)

n <- nrow(payroll)

# 70/15/15 split
train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.70 * n))
remaining <- setdiff(seq_len(n), train_idx)
dev_idx <- sample(remaining, size = floor(0.15 * n))
test_idx <- setdiff(remaining, dev_idx)

train <- payroll[train_idx, ]
dev   <- payroll[dev_idx, ]
test  <- payroll[test_idx, ]

# --- end of data cleaning code from random forest ---

# --- CoPilot-assisted linear model and metrics (a lot of variable names needed to change) ---

#fits a linear model to the training data
model <- lm(Total_Paid ~ 
              Fiscal_Year +
              Regular_Hours + 
              Base_Salary + 
              OT_Hours, data = train)

# test set predictions
predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test)

# test set residuals
res <- test$Total_Paid - predictions

MAE <- mean(abs(res)) # uses this for MAE
RMSE <- sqrt(mean(res^2)) # also usable for root mean squared error

mse_value <- mean((test$Total_Paid - predictions)^2)

r2 <- 1 - sum(res^2) / sum((test$Total_Paid - mean(test$Total_Paid))^2) # standard R^2 (to match random forest)

#summarize model
summary(model)

# print the metrics to paste into the report

print("MAE")
print(MAE)
print("RMSE")
print(RMSE)
print("R^2")
print(r2)

# --- CoPilot-assisted transition block: align csv_data with model and add predictions ---

data_file_path <- "prorated_annual_Citywide_Payroll_Data_(Fiscal_Year)_20260227.csv"

#import the data from the csv file
csv_data <- read.csv(data_file_path, header = TRUE, sep = ",")

# keep only prorated annual rows
csv_data <- csv_data[toupper(trimws(csv_data$Pay.Basis)) == "PRORATED ANNUAL", ]

# clean numeric fields to match model expectations
csv_data$Regular.Gross.Paid <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", csv_data$Regular.Gross.Paid))
csv_data$Total.OT.Paid      <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", csv_data$Total.OT.Paid))
csv_data$Total.OT.Paid[is.na(csv_data$Total.OT.Paid)] <- 0

csv_data$Total.Paid <- csv_data$Regular.Gross.Paid + csv_data$Total.OT.Paid

csv_data$Fiscal.Year   <- as.numeric(as.character(csv_data$Fiscal.Year)) - 2014
csv_data$Regular.Hours <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", csv_data$Regular.Hours))
csv_data$Base.Salary   <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", csv_data$Base.Salary))
csv_data$OT.Hours      <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.-]", "", csv_data$OT.Hours))

# align names with the model
csv_data <- csv_data %>%
  rename(
    Fiscal_Year    = Fiscal.Year,
    Regular_Hours  = Regular.Hours,
    Base_Salary    = Base.Salary,
    OT_Hours       = OT.Hours,
    Total_Paid     = Total.Paid
  )

# add linear-model predictions
csv_data$predicted <- predict(model, newdata = csv_data)

# --- department mean and error analysis taken from old thing ---

# actual department means
actual_means <- csv_data %>%
  group_by(Agency.Name) %>%
  summarise(actual_mean = mean(Total_Paid, na.rm = TRUE))

# predicted department means
predicted_means <- csv_data %>%
  group_by(Agency.Name) %>%
  summarise(predicted_mean = mean(predicted, na.rm = TRUE))

# combine
plot_df <- left_join(actual_means, predicted_means, by = "Agency.Name")

# export scatter plot as pdf for latex
pdf("[plot] linreg_department_means_1.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

#plotting the means
plot(plot_df$actual_mean,
     plot_df$predicted_mean,
     xlab = "Actual Means",
     ylab = "Predicted Means (Linear Model)",
     main = "Prorated Annual Department Means (Lin. Reg.)",
     pch = 19,
     col = "blue")

# add 45-degree line
abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "red")

#close the export
dev.off()

# --- pdf file 2 (employee scatter plot) ---

# actual department means
actual <- csv_data$Total_Paid

# predicted department means
predicted <- predict(model, newdata = csv_data)

# export scatter plot as pdf for latex
pdf("[plot] linreg_employee_plot_1.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

#plotting the points
plot(actual, predicted,
     xlab = "Actual Total Paid (per employee)",
     ylab = "Predicted Total Paid (Linear Model)",
     main = "Prorated Annual Total Paid (Lin. Reg.)",
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.2,
     col = "blue")

# add 45-degree line
abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "red")

#close the export
dev.off()

# --- more analysis ---

# find the top 15 earning departments

plot_df %>%
  mutate(residual = actual_mean - predicted_mean) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(residual))) %>%
  slice(1:15) %>%
  select(Agency.Name, actual_mean, predicted_mean, residual)

library(xtable)

xtable(summary(model))

```
